items.prompt: With fork-join and child threads finishing at t=10 and t=30, the parent resumes at…
items.explain: join waits until every forked thread completes — the maximum time.

items.prompt: fork-join_any with the same durations resumes the parent at…
items.explain: join_any resumes when the first child completes; others may keep running.

choices: Continues immediately while children run in background
Waits for all children before any code after join
items.explain: join_none does not wait; children proceed independently.

items.prompt: After join_any, slower forked threads always stop automatically.
items.explain: Remaining threads may still run unless you disable them explicitly.

items.prompt: You would pick join (not join_none) when…
choices: The parent must observe all child results before continuing
You want the parent to resume at t=0
Children must not consume simulation time
items.explain: join synchronizes on all children finishing.
